assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with MIXED — both additive and reductive signals are present across the test file changes
assertion_results.evidence: The report summary table (report.md line 14) shows: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | Net +3 test functions, +10 assertions; removed test (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`) replaced by stronger behavioral equivalent (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`); 3 new tests in `purl_simplify.rs` |'. The classification is ADDITIVE, not MIXED.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings identify the removed test function test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers as a reductive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 14 mentions the removed test but characterizes it as 'replaced by stronger behavioral equivalent (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)' rather than identifying it as a reductive signal. The overall classification is ADDITIVE, and no reductive findings section exists in the report.

assertion_results.text: The structural summary or reductive findings note the assertion change in test_recommend_purls_basic — the PURL assertion changed from checking a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers to checking a versioned PURL without qualifiers, which the semantic assessment may identify as a relaxation contributing to the MIXED classification
assertion_results.evidence: Criterion-1.md references the assertion 'assert_eq!(body.items[0].purl, "pkg:maven/org.apache/commons-lang3@3.12")' but only describes it as confirming the new behavior, not as a change from a previous assertion that checked a fully qualified PURL with qualifiers. No file in the outputs identifies this assertion change as a relaxation or reductive signal. The report.md does not mention this assertion change at all.

assertion_results.text: The new test file tests/api/purl_simplify.rs is recognized as additive (3 new test functions)
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 14: '3 new tests in `purl_simplify.rs`' listed as part of the Test Change Classification ADDITIVE finding. Criterion-2.md references three test functions from purl_simplify.rs (test_simplified_purl_no_version, test_simplified_purl_mixed_types, test_simplified_purl_ordering_preserved).

assertion_results.text: The new test function test_recommend_purls_dedup in the modified file is recognized as an additive signal
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 14 mentions test_recommend_purls_dedup as part of the overall ADDITIVE classification: 'removed test (`test_recommend_purls_with_qualifiers`) replaced by stronger behavioral equivalent (`test_recommend_purls_dedup`)'. The net count of '+3 test functions' includes this new function as part of the additive total (4 new functions minus 1 removed = net +3).

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files are analysis/report files: report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. No code files (.rs, .toml, etc.) appear in the outputs directory. The report presents findings and analysis only.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md contains no merge commands or auto-merge actions. The report ends with 'Overall: WARN' and an 'Issues Requiring Attention' section listing 4 items for human review. The footer states 'This comment was AI-generated by sdlc-workflow/verify-pr'.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification verdict appears in the verification report summary table and is accompanied by a detailed analysis section explaining the structural and semantic assessment
assertion_results.evidence: The verdict appears in the summary table (report.md line 14): '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE |'. However, there is no separate detailed analysis section explaining structural and semantic assessment. The details are provided only as inline text within the table cell ('Net +3 test functions, +10 assertions; removed test... replaced by stronger behavioral equivalent...; 3 new tests in purl_simplify.rs'). No heading like 'Test Change Classification Analysis' or subsections for structural/semantic assessment exist in the report.

assertion_results.text: The test change classification analysis is based on comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content (function additions, removals, assertion changes) — the structural and semantic assessment references test file content, not acceptance criteria or task requirements (constraint 1.18)
assertion_results.evidence: The report does not contain a structural and semantic assessment section. The Test Change Classification row in the summary table provides summary metrics ('Net +3 test functions, +10 assertions') but does not explicitly reference comparing base-branch and PR-branch file content. No output file describes a comparison of base-branch vs PR-branch test files. The criterion files analyze the PR changes against acceptance criteria, not base-branch vs PR-branch test content.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: Report.md line 13: '| Test Quality | WARN | All test functions documented (PASS); repetitive tests in `purl_simplify.rs` could be parameterized (WARN); Eval Quality: N/A |'. Eval Quality is explicitly N/A. Report.md line 6 confirms: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments on PR |'. The Test Quality combination (WARN) is determined by test documentation and parameterization concerns, unaffected by Eval Quality.
